name: oh-my-project
description: Project-structure GOVERNANCE lane (a third axis — NOT a content domain, NOT a work-style). Route here whenever the work is about WHERE files belong and whether the folder tree obeys its rules, rather than producing the content of any file: classify/place a file under the right folder, detect & safely relocate rule-violating files, codify or update structure/naming rules, register datasets (checksum/split/lineage), bootstrap or audit a project's .omp/ SSOT. The axis is orthogonal to oms/omd: the SAME .pptx is omd when you author its content, omp when you ask whether it sits in the correct folder. Because governance is orthogonal, it is judged FIRST — before both the content domains (oms/omd) and the work-style lanes (omc/sp/omx) — so structure work is never missed and never falls through to handle-directly. The .omp/ index-coherence and safe-fileops guards (move = copy→verify→delete, propose-before-execute, keep STRUCTURE.md/rules.json/DATASETS.md in sync) live only inside omp — so folder governance must not be handled directly or as a raw OMC parallel sweep. Heavy SUB-tasks inside omp (multi-source research, test-first tooling code) may still re-route to omc/sp.

skills.name: project-governance-lane
skills.description: Signals that route to oh-my-project (the session then picks the exact omp-* STAGE: init/codify/organize/dataset/doc/learn/audit).
examples: is this file in the right folder? / 이 파일 제자리에 있어?
이 논문 작업물들이 vault/workspace 분류 기준에 맞아? 어긋난 거 정리해줘
detect rule-violating files and relocate them safely (organize)
register this dataset — checksum, split, lineage (dataset)
audit this folder against its rules — read-only PASS/FAIL (audit)
